# CLAUDE.md — Mi Banquito

> Project intelligence file for AI-assisted development.
> This file is auto-generated by Nous (Developer Package Generator).

## Dev Package — What This Is

This repository is the **dev package** — a ready-to-code Next.js app generated by
the Nous product design pipeline. It is a **single serverless app** (route handlers,
Drizzle, Auth0) — there is no separate backend service or container.

| What | Where | Read when |
|------|-------|-----------|
| **This file (CLAUDE.md)** | Root | Always — architecture rules, current sprint, coding standards |
| **Sprint plan** | `docs/stories/SPRINT_PLAN.md` | Before picking a story — sprints, status, execution order |
| **Story files** | `docs/stories/` | Before coding — full spec: ACs, API contracts, entity changes, screen wiring |
| **Screen specs (TOON)** | `docs/screens/` | For UI — `dataSource` tells you which API to call per section |
| **Architecture** | `docs/specs/09_architecture.md` | For bounded-context structure + API conventions |
| **Decisions** | `docs/decisions/` | When unsure about a design choice |
| **API registry** | `docs/api-contract-registry.json` | Before implementing a route handler — source of truth for API paths |
| **Navigation map** | `docs/specs/07c_navigation_map.json` | **Before adding a route, sidebar item, or flow — authoritative registry** |
| **Feature backlog** | `docs/specs/07b_features_backlog.md` | Feature-level specs (FT-NNN) |

### Dev-guide references (load on demand)

| Topic | File | Load when |

| Commit conventions (US-NNN / CHG-NNN required) | `docs/dev-guide/COMMITS.md` | Writing commit messages |

## Navigation & routes — authoritative source

`docs/specs/07c_navigation_map.json` is the **single source of truth** for routes,
sidebar items, navigation edges, and user flows.

**The invariant**: every `apps/web/src/app/**/page.tsx` must have a matching entry
in `routes[].path` of the nav map. Enforced by `infra/scripts/validate-routes.sh`.
**No exemptions.** Need a new route the map doesn't have? Update the map in Nous
first (via CHG-NNN), sync, then code.

**Sidebar**: `apps/web/src/components/shell/nav-items.gen.ts` is **auto-generated**
from the nav map. Never edit it. After any nav map change, run
`./infra/scripts/regenerate-sidebar.py`. Scaffold a new page with
`./infra/scripts/scaffold-route.sh SCR-NN`. Full guide: `docs/dev-guide/NAVIGATION.md`.

## Project Overview

**Mi Banquito** — built for Organization.

- **Organization:** Organization
- **Owner:** Owner
- **Architecture:** Single serverless Next.js app (App Router + route handlers)
- **Frontend:** nextjs 16 / react / TypeScript
- **Data:** drizzle ORM on postgres  (timestamp-slug migrations)
- **Auth:** auth0 (OIDC / sessions)
- **Hosting:** serverless (Vercel)

## Quick Start

```bash
cd apps/web
pnpm install
pnpm dev          # next dev --webpack
```

Or `task dev` from the repo root.



## Repository Structure

```
apps/
  web/            # nextjs 16 — App Router, route handlers, Drizzle, Auth0
packages/
  db/             # @mi-banquito/db — Drizzle schema + client (drizzle-kit runs here)
  contracts/      # @mi-banquito/contracts — Zod schemas shared client/server
  domain/         # @mi-banquito/domain — business logic
  ui/             # @mi-banquito/ui — shared React components
  config/         # @mi-banquito/config — shared tsconfig / ESLint preset
  design-system/  # tokens + tailwindcss preset (consumed by @mi-banquito/ui)

# Which packages are live vs scaffold, and how to make your first
# delivery: docs/dev-guide/PACKAGE_MAP.md
infra/
  scripts/        # setup, run, seed, reset, sync, sidebar, route scaffolding
docs/
  stories/        # User stories by sprint
  specs/          # ER model, screens, navigation map, architecture
  screens/        # TOON JSON screen specifications (source of truth for UI)
  decisions/      # Product decisions
  dev-guide/      # Security, testing, standards reference (serverless)
```

## App & API Rules

1. **App Router only** — no `pages/` directory. Server Components by default; `"use client"` only when using hooks, events, or browser APIs.
2. **The API is route handlers** — `apps/web/src/app/api/<resource>/route.ts`. There is no separate backend service.
3. **Data via drizzle** — the shared `db` client (import from `@mi-banquito/db`). Every multi-tenant query filters `org_id` (the generated tenant column — there is no `tenant_id`). Reuse tables from `@mi-banquito/db/schema`; derive types with `$inferSelect`/`$inferInsert`.
4. **Auth via auth0** — `auth0.getSession()` in route handlers (no session → 401); `useUser()` + `hasMinRole()` in the UI. Role/tenant come from the verified session, never the request.
5. **Migrations** — `pnpm drizzle-kit`; timestamp-slug files under `packages/db/src/migrations/` named `V<timestamp>__<slug>.sql`. Immutable once applied.
6. **Validation: zod schemas** shared between client and route handlers.
7. **Soft delete is per-table** — only entities that declare a `deleted_at` column have it (check `schema.ts`); most tables hard-delete. Never assume an `is_deleted`/`deleted_at` column exists.
8. **Cursor-based pagination** for list endpoints.
9. **Design tokens from `packages/design-system`** — never hardcode colors, spacing, or fonts.
10. **Client state: Zustand only** — no Redux, no Context for global state.

Full frontend reference: [`docs/dev-guide/FRONTEND.md`](docs/dev-guide/FRONTEND.md).

## Version-Specific Constraints (IMPORTANT)

These rules are derived from the exact framework versions in this project.
Violating them causes compile/test failures. **Read before writing any code.**

### nextjs 16.1.6

- **App Router ONLY** — do NOT create files in `pages/` directory.
- **Server Components by default** — add `"use client"` only when using hooks, event handlers, or browser APIs.
- **Bundler: webpack** — `scripts.build` runs `next build --webpack` (Serwist injects a webpack config; Turbopack would hard-fail). Module imports MUST include file extensions for non-TS files (e.g., `import preset from './tailwind-preset.js'`).
- **Server Actions** available. Use for form submissions instead of API routes.


## Shared Contracts & Entity Conventions

When multiple stories touch the same entity, they MUST use consistent APIs.
**Before writing code, check if the entity already exists** in `packages/db/src/schema.ts`. If it does, reuse its table + inferred types — do NOT redeclare columns or invent a parallel shape.

### Entity Naming Conventions

| Convention | Rule | Example |
|-----------|------|---------|
| Drizzle table object | camelCase | `export const alert = pgTable(...)` |
| DB table name | snake_case | `pgTable("member", {...})` |
| DB columns | snake_case | `org_id`, `created_at` |
| FK columns | `<entity>_id` | `member_id`, `org_id` |
| Tenant column | `org_id` (uuid) | every multi-tenant table carries `org_id` |

### Type & Query Convention

Full example: [`docs/dev-guide/STANDARDS.md`](docs/dev-guide/STANDARDS.md)

- Derive row types from the schema: `type Alert = typeof alert.$inferSelect`. Never hand-write a divergent interface.
- All reads/writes go through the shared `db` client (import from `@mi-banquito/db`) — never a second connection.
- **Tenant isolation:** every query on a multi-tenant table filters by `org_id` (the tenant column generated on every such table). Confirm the column in `packages/db/src/schema.ts` — do NOT invent `tenant_id`.
- **Soft delete is per-table, not universal:** only filter a soft-delete column (e.g. `deleted_at`) on tables that actually declare one in `schema.ts`. Most generated tables hard-delete; do NOT add an `is_deleted` filter to a table that has no such column (it will not type-check).

### Database Migrations (HR-25 — MANDATORY)

This project uses **Drizzle** with **timestamp-slug** migration files under `packages/db/src/migrations/` named `V<UTC-yyyyMMddHHmmss>__<slug>.sql`.

- Edit `packages/db/src/schema.ts`, then regenerate/apply with `pnpm drizzle-kit` (`generate` to author a migration, `push` to apply the schema to a fresh DB).
- Never hand-pick a numeric version; the timestamp is the version. Once applied, a migration file is immutable — add a new one.
- Foreign-key references must point at tables already declared in `schema.ts`.

### Enum Columns (MANDATORY)

- Declare enums once with `pgEnum("role", ["DECISION_MAKER", "INFLUENCER", "USER"])` and reuse the export.
- On-disk values are the exact literals in the `pgEnum` array (case-sensitive) — CHECK/seed values must match them byte-for-byte.
- Never redeclare the same logical enum in two places; import the shared one.

surface is under `apps/web/src/app/(authenticated)/` — one route group per screen.

## Design System

- **Primary:** #007AFF (Primary)
- **Accent:** #FF3B30 (Accent)
- **Font:** Inter (sans), JetBrains Mono (code)
- **Tokens:** `packages/design-system`

## Testing Strategy

**Every story includes tests. Code without tests is not done.**

- Components: render test for every page/component; logic tests for hooks/stores.
- Route handlers: cover auth branches (401/403) + happy path; assert against a test `db`.
- Empty/error/loading states for every screen.
- TDD by default when possible.

Full targets + patterns: [`docs/dev-guide/TESTING.md`](docs/dev-guide/TESTING.md).

## Testing Conventions (MANDATORY)

Full patterns: [`docs/dev-guide/TESTING.md`](docs/dev-guide/TESTING.md)

**Critical gotchas (cause test failures):**
- Exact URL matching in fetch mocks — `/api/v1/socias` also matches `/api/v1/socias/summary` if not exact.
- `beforeEach(() => useMyStore.getState().reset())` — prevent Zustand state leakage between tests.
- `getByRole()` preferred over `getByText()` — more specific and accessible.
- **Server Components** can't use hooks/state — test them as async functions returning JSX; unit-test client logic in the `"use client"` modules.
- **Route handlers** (`apps/web/src/app/api/**/route.ts`): assert against a test Drizzle client or a typed mock of `db` — never the production connection.


## Build Verification Gate

A story is NOT done until the build passes — self-reported "tests pass" is not sufficient.

Before marking any story `done`:
- `pnpm type-check` passes (`tsc --noEmit`, zero errors).
- `pnpm lint` passes (Next.js lint + the design-system token lints).
- `pnpm build` succeeds — runs `next build --webpack` (the serverless/PWA bundler).
- `cd packages/db && pnpm drizzle-kit push && node scripts/verify-schema.mjs` applies the Drizzle schema to a fresh database AND verifies it landed (push exits 0 on an unreachable URL — the verifier fails loud on a silent no-op); no duplicate timestamp migrations under `packages/db/src/migrations/`.
- Report evidence: `{"story":"US-XXX","event":"build_pass",...}` then `{"event":"done",...}`.

Full gate (commands, rejection criteria, troubleshooting):
[`docs/dev-guide/DEFINITION_OF_DONE.md`](docs/dev-guide/DEFINITION_OF_DONE.md).

## API Contract Reconciliation

`docs/api-contract-registry.json` lists every API endpoint the frontend expects,
extracted from TOON `dataSource` fields. Before implementing a route handler, search
the registry and match the path + method **exactly**. After implementing, run
`python3 docs/scripts/nous_api_reconcile.py`. If spec and implementation must
diverge, log it — never silently change paths:

```jsonl
{"story":"US-XXX","event":"deviation","notes":"renamed /api/v1/foo to /api/v1/bar — naming convention"}
```

## Nous Feedback & Story Lifecycle

Append events to `.nous-feedback.jsonl` at the repo root as you work. Core events:
`started`, `ac_pass`, `ac_verify`, `build_pass`, `done`, `blocked`, `deviation`,
`decision`, `feedback`.

```jsonl
{"story":"US-064","event":"started","agent":"<your-agent>"}
{"story":"US-064","event":"build_pass","notes":"type-check + lint + build green"}
{"story":"US-064","event":"done"}
```

**Never mark `done` without adversarial AC verification** — try to BREAK each AC.

Status propagation is operator/CI-owned: your `done` lands in `.nous-feedback.jsonl`,
but `SPRINT_PLAN.md` only refreshes after `nous_package.py sync` runs Nous-side.
Don't re-implement a story just because `SPRINT_PLAN.md` still shows it as backlog —
check `.nous-feedback.jsonl` first. To force a refresh (when you have access):
`./infra/scripts/sync-from-nous.sh`.

Full event schema + AC protocol: [`docs/dev-guide/FEEDBACK.md`](docs/dev-guide/FEEDBACK.md).
Build gate + rejection criteria: [`docs/dev-guide/DEFINITION_OF_DONE.md`](docs/dev-guide/DEFINITION_OF_DONE.md).

## Skills Required

- **Frontend:** TypeScript, react, nextjs 16 App Router, tailwindcss, Zustand, zod
- **Data:** drizzle ORM, postgres 
- **Auth:** auth0 (OIDC / sessions)
- **Tooling:** pnpm, drizzle-kit, Vercel
- **i18n:** locale-aware UI (default `en-US`)
